title: sida vespertina
intro: Sida vespertina, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible seeds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The seeds of the Sida vespertina plant are rich in omega-6 fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has been linked to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved heart health. Sida vespertina can be prepared into herbal supplements, such as capsules or oil, which can be taken internally to support overall health and wellness.
